attached_docs: Pasted text(50).txt — KFM GitHub Repository Documentation Implementation Agent v3.1

validation_gates.reason: Scope is limited to one existing README plus the required generated-work receipt; no executable, schema, policy, workflow, release, lifecycle, or public-surface behavior is changed.

Main advanced from the first inspected commit to 144d10ff4ac87dc37566ad608fe6b002f9250d1a through an unrelated redaction README merge. The target retained blob 6404f3228f307faa7e1e5347a185011e8589f840, so the branch was created from the newer immutable base.

Existing pipelines/catalog placement and document identity were retained. Current Directory Rules, parent README, drift register, adjacent catalog surfaces, open branch/PR overlap, contracts, schemas, ADRs, workflows, and review routing were inspected without creating parallel authority.

The README distinguishes verified documentation and placeholder schema surfaces from unverified shared executables, declarative specs, dedicated tests, CatalogMatrix validator behavior, catalog closure, CI enforcement, and release/publication behavior.
The final README has one H1, one closed KFM Meta Block v2, unique heading slugs, balanced code fences, no trailing whitespace, a final newline, and corrected Mermaid decision-node syntax.
All repository-relative links in the revised README point to files fetched and verified at the pinned base.

Broad pull-request workflows were inspected. Relevant command-bearing workflows are hosted-runner checks; read-only contents permissions were observed where declared. Floating action refs, one workflow without explicit permissions, and lack of catalog-specific enforcement remain disclosed.

Useful v0.1 lifecycle, catalog-output, anti-collapse, gate, receipt, no-network test, correction, rollback, and no-direct-publish obligations were retained. Proposal-as-fact tree claims and a pseudo-schema example were reclassified or removed with an explicit preservation table.

GitHub returned README blob 2c7f919272e7ecc6a713787460f6cd0e781b0641 on commit 320be6d1cbd2205f6f136b3b09d54581d325709f, matching the locally recomputed Git blob SHA and SHA-256 artifact hash 3c3bfaa5b7b0f0a01a2024fd36c87280dc7d89c53cab9249dc7b33b84ac72450.

The connector exposes serialized Contents API writes rather than an expected-head atomic multi-file operation with readable base-tree metadata. README and receipt are written sequentially on the scoped review branch with remote read-back.
The API-only workflow did not clone or execute repository code, and no shared catalog command was verified. Pull-request checks are observed separately and are never inferred as passing.
The receipt payload was validated locally against the required fields, enums, hash patterns, date-time formats, and additional-property restrictions of the fetched generated-receipt schema.
Human review remains pending and separate from generation, static validation, and mergeability.

emitter: OpenAI GPT-5.6 Thinking via ChatGPT GitHub connector
notes: Task kfm-pipelines-catalog-readme-v0-2-20260719. Documentation-only update. This receipt records provenance and bounded validation outcomes; it is not factual proof, policy approval, human review, release approval, lifecycle promotion, or publication authority.
